task_description:
Setup user authentication end-to-end using Supabase Auth with Google OAuth on the Render-hosted FastAPI website. Requirements:

1. LOGIN BUTTON: Add 'Login' button at top-right of home screen (website/static/index.html)
2. GOOGLE OAUTH: Login by Google using Supabase Auth (PKCE flow, supabase-js CDN client)
3. FLEXIBLE ARCHITECTURE: Design so other OAuth providers (GitHub, Twitter) can be added later
4. SUPABASE SCHEMA: Create user_auth schema in supabase/website/user_auth/ folder
5. FEATURE FOLDER: Create website/features/user_auth/ with self-contained auth assets
6. UPDATE ABOUT: Add user_auth to website/features/About.md
7. SESSION MANAGEMENT: Persist user session, show avatar/name after login
8. LOGOUT: Add logout functionality
9. PERFORMANCE: Full auth flow must complete under 5 seconds (click login -> select Google account -> allow permissions -> logged in)
10. SERVER-SIDE: FastAPI JWT validation dependency for protected API routes
11. USER LINKING: Link Supabase auth.users to existing kg_users table (replace hardcoded 'default-web-user')

Critical constraints:
- Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS frontend (no React/Vue/npm)
- No purple in UI (use teal accent for main site)
- Supabase MCP not available this session; use SQL files for schema, manual dashboard config for Google OAuth provider
- Must work on Render.com deployment (port 10000, ephemeral filesystem)

supabase_project.kg_users_render_user_id: TEXT column, currently 'default-web-user', designed for auth provider IDs
